text: delegates, then we have the decision, we have the issue of our government, the secretary, we make the necessary arrangements for the meeting in one year's time. All right. So there's no other business to be discussed. As there's no other business to be discussed, our nine gave the food to the secretary, general to give us his closing remarks. And then the meeting will be at turn for one year. Ask him please. Thank you. Thank you, Chair. The distinguished delegates, I'm going to start the session by, first of all, thanking the Chair for the work he has carried out, the matter in which he has handled the meeting, his efforts, and in particular, at the end, he didn't need the ambulance. Secondly, I want to thank my team in the secretary, because they have worked, they have worked really hard. To support you during this week. The Marine Environment Division, the new director David, I normally don't do this, but Deputy Director Tim B, the head of Section Wu, the rest of the team from Camille, DG, lay back the rest of the division, the legal division led by the director, the other, the conference division, all the work that they have done. We stay here just to serve you, and I'm telling you, they are very dedicated, and I hope you have noticed that. I don't have a lot to say to you right now, and that doesn't happen often. You always look for the seat of our line and sit things. When you were making your interview, you never mentioned this week, even though you have different, different opinions, you all spoke in support of the work of this organization. Particularly this committee, I will ask you to reflect on this session. The outcome is your decision. You know that I will always respect that, and I will stand the secretary at the works with me as well as the chair. But it's in the manner in which the conversations took place this week. This is not the normal I'm meeting. And I know that it's a sensitive topic for many, an important topic for all. And the reason why I ask you not to clap is because for me, there are no winners and losers in this session. But it's the time to really look back at how we have approached this meeting. How now you have one year, because you will continue work on several aspects of these amendments. You have one year to truly talk to each other, negotiate and come to that consensus agreements that you all make reference to. I know that voting is part of the process, but I will encourage you to go on consensus. So as we come to an end, my plea to you is not to repeat the way that we have approached this meeting. In other discussions, of future discussions, of this of any other meeting at AIMO, it doesn't help your organization, it doesn't help yourself. But to continue to be proactive, to facilitate this moment to learn from these, have the conversations come back fresh in one year, ready to negotiate and to take the next steps forward that you consider appropriate for the implementation, or to address the goals that were set up in the 2023 strategy that you all agreed to. You are nice and kind to each other, it doesn't cost much. So as we come to an end, I will ask you for one favor. A favor. I know I lose in my voice and... Let's not celebrate. Okay? Are we concerned that we need to address? Let's work with each other. I believe in you. I will defend you outside you always know that, and I will continue to do it. But for that, I need your help as well. Thank you all for your time, Chair. All right. From my end, I see your Chair. This was an incredibly interesting meeting, Extra-Odenario Session, indeed, and Extra-Odenario Session, and most of you know me, you know my position when it comes to the whole issue of G&G, the proponent of the five C's, called the call of five C's, the first one is where compromise is, second one is consensus, collaboration, cooperation and commitment. You need that more than ever before. Talk to one another, and listen to one another. Does that mean to talk, but also listen to the other person's view? This is not a winner. There is no winner, there is no loser. It's important that you do not lose sight of the fact where this strategy was crafted in the first place. To address a serious problem, an existential problem that we are facing. I'd like to thank the Secretary of the Director and his team, Secretary General, the Interpreter of the Security, Ketross, everybody. I thank you all so much. And I have an announcement, I'm told that this room is going to be prepared for the next meeting next week, so please do not leave any of your items in here leaving. Take them with you, because they need to start preparing the room immediately for the next meeting next week. With that, I give the food to Ignatikino. Thank you very much, Chair. As many of you know, there is a tradition in this organization, since we're speaking about traditions, for the UK, as the host government to say thanks, on behalf of their distinguished delegates attending in person and online. Thank you very much, Conference Division, IT and Security, Ketoring, Interpretes. Thank you to the Chair of the Working Group and drafting group for the excellent guide that they gave us. Thank you, Chair, for your leadership. You have had a tremendous hard job, did not need any audiences there or otherwise. And you have done it will calm, good manners and consideration. Thank you. Thank you also to the Secretary General, Director of Marine Environment and Secretaryate, for all the tremendously hard work during this process, chairs of working groups and drafting groups. And I would like to thank members for the long hours that went into developing the texts that we considered and the constructive spirit of engagement throughout the process. As we head out this weekend, I hope we reflect on all the good things that happen in the room, productive conversations, and we think what we can achieve next. And let us remember, above all, we do serve our sector. Thank you. Thank you, United Kingdom. That this meeting is in a place. It's just a few minutes. Is there a problem? Yes, but we want to integrate something. Okay, like how many? I mean, in the environment. No problem. But in that area? Okay. Thank you. Thank you.
